title: NSW EPA – Food Rescue Grants
summary: Supports organisations expanding food rescue and relief capacity to safely manage increasing amounts of rescued food in NSW.
best_for: food rescue; food relief; community kitchens; redistribution logistics; cold storage; rescued food systems
who_can_apply: Food rescue and/or relief organisations and coordinating organisations such as not-for-profits, NSW councils, NSW local government regional waste groups or regional organisations of councils.
notes: Closed at time of review. Strong fit for community food access, redistribution, and rescued-food infrastructure.
source: Compiled from NSW EPA Food Rescue Grants page, reviewed April 2026.

title: Community Building Partnership 2026
summary: NSW Government grants for small local infrastructure projects that strengthen community wellbeing.
best_for: community infrastructure; sheds; equipment; accessibility upgrades; gardens; shared spaces
who_can_apply: Eligible local community organisations and councils including incorporated associations, public companies limited by guarantee, Local Aboriginal Land Councils, Indigenous corporations, NSW local councils, section 355 committees, ACNC-registered trusts, and registered P&C Associations.
notes: Open at time of review. Good fit where a project needs physical infrastructure rather than just program costs.
source: Compiled from Community Building Partnership 2026 page, reviewed April 2026.

title: NSW Environmental Trust – Environmental Education
summary: Funds projects that build skills and drive behaviour change to improve environmental outcomes.
best_for: environmental education; workshops; community behaviour change; sustainability training; citizen learning
funding: Tier 1: up to $60,000 per project; Tier 2: $60,001 to $250,000 per project
who_can_apply: Community groups, not-for-profit organisations, state and local government, non-commercial cooperatives, councils, universities, regional organisations of councils, NSW local Aboriginal Land Councils, and registered NSW Aboriginal Corporations.
notes: Environmental Trust annual grants page lists next round opening on 21 Sep 2026 and closing on 9 Nov 2026.
source: Compiled from NSW Environmental Trust grants pages, reviewed April 2026.

title: NSW Environmental Trust – Environmental Restoration and Rehabilitation
summary: Supports projects that protect, conserve and restore the NSW environment.
best_for: habitat restoration; landcare; regeneration; riparian work; biodiversity; on-ground environmental repair
who_can_apply: State and local government and community groups.
notes: Environmental Trust annual grants page lists the 2026 round opening on 1 Apr 2026 and closing on 13 May 2026.
source: Compiled from NSW Environmental Trust grants pages, reviewed April 2026.

title: NSW Environmental Trust – Protecting Our Places
summary: Encourages and empowers Aboriginal communities to protect, conserve and restore landscapes and waterways important to them.
best_for: Aboriginal-led environmental care; cultural landscapes; waterways; restoration; Country-based projects
who_can_apply: NSW Aboriginal community organisations or groups.
notes: Environmental Trust annual grants page lists next round opening on 15 June 2026 and closing on 31 July 2026.
source: Compiled from NSW Environmental Trust grants pages, reviewed April 2026.

title: Community Resilience Grants Program
summary: Supports local Northern Rivers not-for-profit organisations providing critical services and recovery support after the 2022 floods.
best_for: flood recovery; community connection; food support; wellbeing; outreach; resilience services
who_can_apply: Eligible not-for-profit organisations delivering flood recovery and/or resilience services to people in the 7 Northern Rivers LGAs, with annual gross revenue below $5 million.
notes: Closed program, but highly relevant as a reference point for future Northern Rivers resilience funding.
source: Compiled from NSW Community Resilience Grants Program page, reviewed April 2026.

title: NSW EPA – Business Food Waste Partnership Grants
summary: Supports organisations working with food-wasting businesses to reduce and source-separate food waste ahead of NSW FOGO requirements.
best_for: food waste reduction; hospitality engagement; institutional kitchens; education; organics systems; circular economy
who_can_apply: Peak bodies, sector leaders, councils, educators and other relevant organisations working with food-wasting businesses.
notes: Useful for larger-scale behaviour change or sector support projects linked to food waste and organics recovery.
source: Compiled from NSW EPA grant and news pages, reviewed April 2026.
